prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
French
Le silence entre nous en disait plus que n'aurait pu le faire n'importe quelle dispute.

Note: I used "en disait plus" + "dispute" to keep the metaphorical sense of "silence said more." "Dispute" (arguing/row) matches the English "argument"; "débat" would be milder.

Japanese
二人の間の沈黙は、どんな議論よりも雄弁だった。

Note: I used 「雄弁だった」("was eloquent") to capture the idiom "said more" naturally. 「議論」 is a neutral "argument/discussion"; 「口論／言い争い」 would emphasize a harsher quarrel.

Arabic
كان الصمت بيننا أبلغَ من أيِّ جدالٍ.

Note: "أبلغَ" (more expressive/eloquent) conveys the idea of "said more" idiomatically. "جدال" fits "argument"; alternatives like "شجار" would imply a more physical/angry fight.
